data.title: CyberCube Appoints Chris Methven as New CEO
data.description:
CyberCube, a cyber risk modeling and analytics company, announced on February 9, 2026, the appointment of Chris Methven as the new Chief Executive Officer (CEO). 

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Methven succeeds Pascal Millaire, who has served as CEO and led the company's development from its establishment within Symantec in 2015 through its transition to a standalone entity in 2018. 

Methven Steps Up After Six Years as Chief Growth Officer

Chris Methven joined CyberCube in April 2020 and has served as Chief Growth Officer (CGO) for the past six years. In that role, he oversaw commercial strategy and client engagement across the company's cyber risk analytics platform. Before joining CyberCube, Methven was Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) at Achilles, where he oversaw global sales, marketing, account management, operations, and customer programs.

"Chris has a proven track record as Chief Growth Officer at CyberCube. His deep knowledge of the business means this transition will be seamless for our people and our clients," Scott G. Stephenson, Chair of CyberCube's Board of Directors, stated in a press release. The cybersecurity firm stated the appointment marks the next phase of its development, following a period of growth under Millaire, who will continue to support the company as a Senior Advisor. 

New CEO Appointment Follows $180 Million Capital Raise

The new CEO appointment comes months after CyberCube secured more than $180 million in investment led by Spectrum Equity. In a LinkedIn post addressing the transition, Millaire stated that, with a new board in place and the recent capital raise, CyberCube is well-positioned to strengthen its long-term leadership as a cyber analytics partner in the P&C insurance industry.

The company serves more than 130 clients across the cyber (re)insurance value chain. Its solutions are used by 75% of the top 40 U.S. and European cyber insurers by gross written premiums, as well as a majority of the top 20 global brokers. The executive transition maintains leadership continuity as CyberCube scales its position within the global cyber insurance analytics market.

data.title: Magnificent 7 Wipes Out 2026 Gains as Tech Concerns Heighten
data.description:
Every member of the Magnificent 7, which makes up roughly one-third of the S&P 500 (SPX), has posted a negative return for 2026 as of Friday's close.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The disappointing performance comes amid fears of overspending in the AI industry, as well as the effects of the technology on future sales and margins. Microsoft (MSFT) has been hit the hardest with a loss of 17%, while chip leader Nvidia (NVDA) has been the least affected within the group with a loss of about 2%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: OpenAI and Grok In the Firing Line as U.K. Government Ramps Up Child Safety Laws
data.description:
AI chatbot operators such as OpenAI and X have been warned by the British government that they no longer have a "free pass" when it comes to online safety rules.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Illegal or Harmful Content

The U.K. government said today that chatbot providers including OpenAI's ChatGPT and X's Grok, would be responsible for preventing them from generating illegal or harmful content. That would extend rules that currently apply only to content shared between users on social media sites like Meta's (META) Facebook.

The chatbot crackdown follows the furor over Elon Musk's xAI chatbot Grok, which was used to create sexualized deep fake images of women and children.

The U.K. government plans to amend the Crime and Policing Bill, which will require AI chatbot providers to comply with duties under the Online Safety Act to protect users from illegal content. Failure to do so could result in fines and other penalties.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: U.S. Dollar Sentiment at Weakest Level Since 2012, Shows BofA Survey
data.description:
The U.S. Dollar Index, which measures the dollar's value against six major currencies, has fallen by 1.2% year-to-date. More downside could be on the way, according to a Bank of America survey of fund managers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The survey showed that dollar positioning was the most negative since 2012, the year when the bank started to collect data for the statistic.

Weaker Dollar Could Benefit U.S. Growth and Corporate Profits

The falling sentiment comes alongside concerns over the Fed's independence and heightened geopolitical tensions, which could shake confidence in the U.S. economy and reduce foreign demand for the dollar.

"We still see an environment where the dollar can weaken from here," said JPMorgan Asset Management's Iain Stealey.

At the same time, a weaker dollar has benefits as well, such as boosting U.S. exports, increasing earnings for multinational businesses, and lifting gross domestic product (GDP) growth.

data.title: 3 'Strong-Buy' Tech Stocks Top Analysts Love Despite AI Fears
data.description:
Amid growing AI fears and a broader market pullback, three technology stocks rated "Strong Buys" by top analysts shine brightly. The sector surges ahead, fueled by breakthroughs in artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing, semiconductors, and cybersecurity. Investors seeking long-term growth should consider adding exposure to this thriving space for significant potential upside. That said, with sky-high valuations and talk of an AI bubble, investors must make decisions after rigorous research and insights from reliable analysts.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Top Wall Street Analysts, each stock received a new Buy rating recently and has significant upside as well. Click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio.

Adyen (ADYYF)

Adyen is a Dutch fintech firm, offering an integrated end-to-end platform that handles payment acceptance, processing, and settlement across online, mobile, and physical channels. UBS analyst Justin Forsythe reiterated his Buy rating but trimmed his price target from $2,000 to $1,600, implying 45.7% upside potential. Adyen posted in-line second-half fiscal 2025 results but issued weak guidance for revenue and payment processing volume. Over the past three months, five Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy, while one analyst has rated it a Hold. Taken together, their 12-month average Adyen price target implies an upside of more than 58%.

Snowflake (SNOW)

Snowflake is a cloud-based data platform that unifies data warehousing, data lakes, data engineering, data science, and data sharing into a single service. Snowflake is set to release its Q4FY26 results on February 25. UBS analyst Karl Keirstead maintained his Buy rating but lowered his price target from $310 to $265, implying 45.4% upside potential. Over the past three months, 21 Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy, while two analysts have rated it a Hold. Taken together, their 12-month average Snowflake price target implies an upside of more than 51%.

Datadog (DDOG)

Datadog provides cloud-scale monitoring and analytics for IT infrastructure, applications, and security. After beating Q4FY25 sales and earnings estimates, Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz reiterated his Buy rating and $170 price target, implying 35.8% upside potential. Over the last three months, 20 Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy, while one analyst has rated it a Sell. Taken together, their 12-month average Datadog price target implies an upside of nearly 47%.

Who Are the Top Analysts? 

TipRanks ranks financial analysts according to the success rates of their ratings and the average return on each of their ratings. The Top Analysts have each earned a five-star ranking, thanks to the accuracy and profitability of their ratings over time.

data.title: 'A Fork in the Road,' Says Investor About Micron Stock
data.description:
It has been a whopping ride northbound for Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), as the company has taken off over the past few months. All told, MU has seen its share price catapult by more than 230% during the last half year.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The rising share price is more than just unfounded exuberance. The company's fiscal Q1 2026 revenue of $13.64 billion was a significant sequential jump from the $11.32 billion it reported in fiscal Q4 2025 (and an even bigger increase from the $8.71 billion it delivered in fiscal Q1 2025).

Moreover, Micron's operating cash flow of $8.41 billion wasn't even in the same ballpark as the $3.24 billion it reported in fiscal Q1 2025.

So, with all the great news, why isn't an investment in MU an open-and-shut case? That would be the fears that Micron's experience will be cyclical in nature, and that the seemingly limitless growth of some of the other names from the AI space won't be in the company's future.

Investor Keith Speights is throwing himself into this weighty debate, posing the question that inquiring minds want to know.

"Will Micron be the next Intel? Or will it be the next Nvidia? My answer is neither. Micron will be the next Micron," states the 5-star investor.

Speights acknowledges that no company can escape the law of supply and demand, and Micron is no exception. And Micron, he emphasizes, is a cyclical stock.

However, Speights also points out that this time could be different, as Micron's technology is "invaluable" (in the words of Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, no less) to many of the AI advances taking place.

"I suspect that the demand for AI (and, therefore, the demand for Micron's HBM) will continue for much longer than the memory chip cycles over the last few decades," adds Speights.

That gives the investor enough confidence to predict that MU will be taking the more lucrative fork in the road.

"If I'm right, Micron could be a lot more like Nvidia than Intel going forward," concludes Speights. (To watch Keith Speights' track record, click here)

Wall Street is also banking on continued growth, with a caveat. MU's 26 Buys and 2 Holds combine to give it a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, it seems the gains have shot up faster than some of the bulls had anticipated, as its 12-month average price target implies minimal downside. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Jobs Could be the Latest Victim of AI, Cautions Mohamed El-Erian
data.description:
January's jobs report was a welcome sight for many, showing 130,000 nonfarm payroll additions and the unemployment rate falling to 4.3% from 4.4%. However, cracks are starting to emerge underneath the surface, according to Allianz Chief Economic Adviser Mohamed El-Erian.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

El-Erian points out that the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) revised nonfarm payrolls for December and November lower by a combined 99,000 jobs. In addition, the BLS revised total payroll gains in 2025 to 181,000 from 584,000, marking the lowest level of hiring outside of a recession since 2003.

U.S. Jobs Decouple from GDP

At the same time, the U.S. economy continues to grow at a healthy pace, demonstrating a decoupling of job growth from gross domestic product (GDP).

"There are also reasons to believe that this period of decoupling of employment from growth may prove more persistent and more consequential," said El-Erian. These reasons include the onset of AI and a widespread affordability crisis. With strong economic growth and a weak labor market, wealth inequality could widen even further, raising the risk of social and economic tensions.

data.title: Prepare for the 'Dark Side of AI,' Warns Wall Street
data.description:
The boom in AI has created several side effects that have caught many investors by surprise. For example, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) is down by 19% year-to-date amid concerns that AI will intensify competition and lower sales within the industry. Last week, the freight industry was the latest to be hit after Algorhythm Holdings (RIME) announced that its AI-driven SemiCab platform had scaled freight volumes by 300% to 400% without increasing headcount.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"That's the dark side of AI," Innovator Capital Management Chief Investment Strategist Tim Urbanowicz told Yahoo Finance. "We need to pay attention to that because I do think there's going to be other industries that are disrupted, and this is certainly a threat."

AI Uncertainty Pressures Stocks, Although Winners Could Emerge

AI has raised uncertainty about future cash flows, lowering stock valuations in the process. At the same time, companies that are able to use AI as a tool to innovate their core businesses could "benefit, especially those in the financials and health care sectors," said UBS Global Wealth Management CIO Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi.

While several sectors have experienced poor performance in 2026, the S&P 500 (SPX) is down by less than 1%, demonstrating strong market breadth. Urbanowicz expects the benchmark index to end the year at 7,600, citing tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill and a favorable regulatory environment.
